records.ety: [Prov. E. titter to tremble, to seesaw; cf. Icel. titra to tremble, OHG. zittarōn, G. zittern.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Teetered (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Teetering.]

defs: To move up and down on the ends of a balanced plank, or the like, as children do for sport; to seesaw; to titter; to titter-totter.
quotes: [The bobolink] alit upon the flower, and teetered up and down.
